verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' with url='https://github.com/sendgrid'. However, this is SendGrid's own GitHub organization hosting their SDKs/libraries, NOT a dedicated MCP server. Evidence from apio.sh mentions 'an official MCP server' but provides no link or concrete evidence proving it exists. The EVIDENCE does not show a dedicated MCP server repo/implementation, so exists should be 'unknown' (if apio.sh claim is unverified) or 'no' (if we treat the GitHub link as incorrect). Dropping the url.
https://github.com/sendgrid is SendGrid's official SDK/library org, not an MCP server. This URL should be removed.
Evidence confirms API key (Bearer token) as primary method and Basic auth with 'apikey' username for legacy/SMTP services. Username/password basic auth deprecated Q4 2020. Details match the record.
Evidence confirms self-serve API key creation in dashboard. The 60-day trial is self-serve, and paid plans (starting $19.95/mo) are also self-serve per apio.sh. 'self-serve-trial' is appropriate.
Evidence confirms: free tier replaced with 60-day trial (100 emails/day) as of May 27, 2025; paid plan required after trial; 2FA mandatory. Details match record.
Evidence confirms REST API v3, broad coverage (mail send, templates, contacts, marketing, stats, API keys, etc.), and 7 language SDKs. Type='REST' breadth='broad' is accurate.
Self-serve API key creation, hosted REST API, trial available immediately—developers can start calling the API now. 'buildable-now' is correct.
No blocker preventing API access; trial is self-serve and API is callable immediately. null is correct.
All evidence URLs are reachable and support the claims in the record (auth methods, trial plan, pricing, API surface).
0.8 is reasonable given strong evidence for auth, pricing, API surface, and buildability. The only uncertainty is around 'official MCP server' mentioned in apio.sh without proof.
Flags are accurate: free tier removed May 2025, 2FA required, and multiple unofficial MCP implementations (no single official one confirmed per evidence).

revised.name: SendGrid
revised.one_liner: SendGrid provides a broad, well-documented REST API (v3) for transactional/marketing email, authenticated via self-serve API keys, with multiple community MCP server implementations already available.

auth_methods.notes: Primary method: API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. Created via account settings UI (app.sendgrid.com/settings/api_keys). Permissions can be scoped per key.
Supported for some legacy services using 'apikey' as username and the API key as password (SMTP relay). Username/password basic auth was deprecated as of Q4 2020.

revised.self_serve_notes: Developers can self-serve create API keys immediately from the dashboard. However, as of May 27, 2025, SendGrid replaced its permanent free tier with a one-time 60-day free trial (100 emails/day) for new accounts; after trial expiry a paid plan (starting at $19.95/mo) is required to continue sending/using API features. Two-factor authentication is mandatory on the account.

api_surface.notes: SendGrid v3 REST API with broad coverage: mail send, templates, contacts/marketing campaigns, suppression lists, stats, API key management, etc. SDKs available in 7 languages.

evidence.supports: API key via Bearer token is the primary auth method for v3 API; self-serve key creation in account settings.
Details on Bearer API key auth, Basic auth with 'apikey' username, mandatory 2FA.
Free plan replaced with 60-day trial as of May 27, 2025.
Trial account plan details, 100 emails/day for 60 days.
